scenarios.requirement: Sequential allocation returns strictly increasing values starting at 1
scenarios.assertion: Next() called N times in a single goroutine returns 1, 2, 3, ..., N (each == previous+1, first == 1)

scenarios.requirement: Concurrent allocation from many goroutines yields exactly that many unique IDs, no duplicates, no lost updates
scenarios.assertion: 100 goroutines x 100 Next() calls (10,000 total) collected under -race; len(collected) == 10000 and len(dedupe-set(collected)) == 10000

scenarios.requirement: Persisting the allocator state and reopening restores the correct next-ID high-water-mark, no collision with previously allocated IDs
scenarios.assertion: After allocating up to maxID on FileManager/IDAllocator instance #1, closing, then opening a brand-new FileManager/IDAllocator instance #2 on the same underlying path, the first Next() call on instance #2 returns exactly maxID+1 (strictly greater than every previously allocated ID, no gap, no collision)
